title: nymphaea alba subsp. biradiata
intro: Nymphaea alba subsp. biradiata, also known as the white water lily or fragrant white water lily, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Nymphaeaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant's roots and flowers are believed to have health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the roots and flowers of Nymphaea alba subsp. biradiata can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
